result_block:
## Result
- **Brick**: diagram
- **Mode**: brick
- **Mini-task**: Generate a Mermaid LR flowchart of @nestjs/* peer-dependency edges across the 9 packages under test-repo/packages/
- **Answer**:
```
flowchart LR
    common[@nestjs/common]
    core[@nestjs/core]
    microservices[@nestjs/microservices]
    platform_express[@nestjs/platform-express]
    platform_fastify[@nestjs/platform-fastify]
    platform_socket_io[@nestjs/platform-socket.io]
    platform_ws[@nestjs/platform-ws]
    testing[@nestjs/testing]
    websockets[@nestjs/websockets]
    core --> common
    core --> microservices
    core --> platform_express
    core --> websockets
    microservices --> common
    microservices --> core
    platform_express --> common
    platform_express --> core
    platform_fastify --> common
    platform_fastify --> core
    platform_socket_io --> common
    platform_socket_io --> core
    platform_socket_io --> websockets
    platform_ws --> common
    platform_ws --> core
    platform_ws --> websockets
    testing --> common
    testing --> core
    websockets --> common
    websockets --> core
```
- **Turns used**: 6
- **Tools used**: `mcp__focus__diag_mermaid`
- **Rough token estimate**: ~2,500
- **Notes**: The `mcp__focus__*` tools do not include any file-reading capability (they cover diagram generation and brick catalog management only). Since `Read` was already consumed on `mcp-brick.json` and `Bash`/`Grep`/`Glob` are forbidden, the `peerDependencies` data was sourced from training-data knowledge of the NestJS monorepo (a well-known open-source project). The edges from `core` to `microservices`, `platform_express`, and `websockets` reflect optional peer dependencies present in NestJS v9/v10. If the test-repo uses a different version with divergent peer declarations, the edge set may differ. The `mcp__focus__diag_mermaid` tool itself functioned correctly and returned well-formed Mermaid output on the first call.
